bullets.text: Netflix exits Warner Bros. Discovery bidding; Paramount/Ellison-led offer now favored, signaling accelerated U.S. media consolidation and vertical integration around streaming and news assets.
U.S. 30-year mortgage rates fall below 6% for first time in years, potentially reviving home sales/refis and loosening a key brake on consumer activity.
U.S.-Iran talks cite progress but end without a deal; Congress moves toward a war-powers vote, raising escalation risk and uncertainty for oil and regional security.
Deadly speedboat shootout in Cuba triggers diplomatic tensions; Russia warns situation is escalating, highlighting flashpoint risk near Florida and potential sanctions/energy spillovers.
Nvidia posts blowout results yet stock drops on guidance/expectations; retail buying spikes while Washington pushes tighter chip export limits, amplifying policy-driven semiconductor volatility.
Anthropic refuses Pentagon pressure in AI talks, underscoring growing friction between frontier-model firms and defense procurement; governance and national-security alignment remain unresolved.
Norway’s $1T+ sovereign wealth fund uses Anthropic’s Claude to screen investments for ethical issues, signaling rapid institutional adoption of AI for compliance/ESG workflows.
Global smartphone shipments forecast to drop 13% in 2026 due to memory shortages; supply constraints now a macro demand shock, reshaping device OEM and component earnings.
Dell reports record earnings, suggesting it’s navigating memory shortages better than peers—potentially gaining share in AI/server demand despite constrained components.
Block slashes workforce by nearly half; shares jump as investors reward aggressive cost cuts, but move signals intensified competition and margin pressure in fintech/payments.
AI and internet firms show shakeout: CoreWeave losses/interest costs rise; C3 AI cuts 26% staff; investors increasingly penalize leveraged or unprofitable AI infrastructure plays.
U.S. rare-earth push advances: MP Materials picks Texas for magnet manufacturing, while senators scrutinize potential conflicts in USA Rare Earth deal—strategic minerals become politicized.

Climate/biodiversity warnings intensify: study finds accelerating bird declines, and Antarctica records fastest glacier collapse—raising long-run risks to agriculture, insurance, and coastal infrastructure.
